sections.paragraph:
φιλόνεικος, ον (also-νικος; on the spelling s. φιλονεικία; s. νεῖκος).

① quarrelsome, contentious (Pind., Pla., et al.; M. Ant. 5, 35; Ezk 3:7; Philo; Jos., C. Ap. 1, 160, Ant. 15, 166) 1 Cor 11:16.

② in a favorable sense (X., Pla., Plut.; Philo, Det. Pot. Ins. 3, Aet. Min 104; Jos., Ant. 15, 156 al.) emulous, (in) eager (rivalry) φιλόν(ε)ικοι ἔστε καὶ ζηλωταὶ περὶ τῶν ἀνηκόντων εἰς σωτηρίαν 1 Cl 45:1.—DELG s.v. νίκη.

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

  1. Ant. M. Ant. = Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1058
